q: The size or the diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of the _______.
The lens near the object in a compound microscope is called the _______.
A simple microscope consists of a single _______ lens of short focal length.
The astronomical telescope consists of two _______ lenses.
In a camera, the _______ is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The _______ acts as a screen on which the image is formed in the eye.
Myopia is corrected by using a _______ lens.
In normal adjustment of a telescope, the final image is formed at _______.
The magnifying power of a telescope in normal adjustment is M = _______.
Hypermetropia is corrected by using a _______ lens.
The distance of the near point for a normal eye is about _______.
The image formed on the film of a camera is real and _______.
In a compound microscope, the final image is _______ with respect to the first object.
The power of a lens is expressed in _______.
In a simple microscope, the object is placed between the focus and the _______.
The process by which the focal length of the eye lens is changed is called _______.
The image formed by a simple microscope is virtual, erect and _______.
In a camera, the _______ is used to control the time for which light falls on the film.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is the _______ of the magnifications of the objective and the eyepiece.
The separation of the two lenses of an astronomical telescope in normal adjustment is L = _______.
A _______ telescope is used to provide an erect image of a distant object.
The magnifying power of a simple microscope is M = 1 + _______.
The focal length of the objective lens is _______ than the focal length of the eyepiece lens in a compound microscope.
In a compound microscope, the first image must fall within the _______ of the eyepiece.
The crystalline lens of the eye is a _______ lens.
In a myopic eye, the image of a distant object is formed _______ the retina.
In a hypermetropic eye, the image of a nearby object is formed _______ the retina.
The power of a lens P is the reciprocal of its _______.
When photographing in bright light a _______ f-number must be used.
When photographing in dim light a _______ aperture must be used.
The _______ telescope uses a diverging lens as the eyepiece.
In a camera, the diaphragm is placed in _______ of the convex lens.
The eye is very similar to a _______ in its optical function.
The image formed on the retina is real and _______.
Spectacles are optical instruments employing _______.
The objective lens of an astronomical telescope has a _______ focal length.
In a compound microscope, the first image is real, inverted and _______.
The magnification of the objective of a compound microscope is mo = _______.
The focal length of the objective lens is much _______ than that of the eyepiece in an astronomical telescope.
In an astronomical telescope, the first image is formed at the _______ of the objective.
The image formed by the objective of an astronomical telescope is real and _______.
The eyepiece of an astronomical telescope acts as a _______ microscope.
The final image in a Galilean telescope is _______.
A terrestrial telescope uses an _______ lens to produce an erect image.
The distance between the two lenses in a Galilean telescope is L = _______.
The lens near the eye in any optical instrument is called the _______.
For a normal eye, the _______ point is at infinity.
In a camera, focusing is done by moving the _______.
The _______ is the least distance at which an eye can see an object clearly.
The objective of a compound microscope has a _______ focal length fo.
